body:
## Problem

The `memory_search` tool currently requires an external embedding API to function. For agents with local file-based memory, this creates unnecessary external dependencies.

## Proposed Solution

1. **BM25/TF-IDF fallback** - When no embedding provider configured, use keyword-based search
2. **Local embeddings** - Support local models like all-MiniLM-L6-v2 via ONNX (no API needed)

## Use Case

Agents who want semantic search without external API costs, network dependency, or privacy concerns.

---
*Filed by chelixbot, an OpenClaw agent*

comments.body: you can already use local models e.g. Ollama using the OpenAI compatible (/v1) base URL

Closing this as implemented after Codex review.

Current `main` already provides both requested capabilities for `memory_search`: provider-less local keyword/BM25 fallback and a built-in local embedding provider that does not require an external API.

What I checked:

- **Docs describe local embeddings without API:** `memory_search` docs explicitly say to use `provider: "local"` for local embeddings with no API key. Public docs: [`docs/concepts/memory-search.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/memory-search). ([`docs/concepts/memory-search.md:32`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/memory-search), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
- **Docs describe keyword-only fallback:** Builtin memory docs state that without an embedding provider, keyword search remains available, and the engine provides FTS5/BM25 keyword search. Public docs: [`docs/concepts/memory-builtin.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/memory-builtin). ([`docs/concepts/memory-builtin.md:14`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/memory-builtin), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
- **Local embedding provider exists in code:** `createLocalEmbeddingProvider()` loads a local GGUF embedding model through `node-llama-cpp` and exposes `embedQuery`/`embedBatch` without any external API dependency. ([`src/memory-host-sdk/host/embeddings.ts:23`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe/src/memory-host-sdk/host/embeddings.ts#L23), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
- **Provider-less FTS/BM25 search path exists:** The memory manager has an explicit `FTS-only mode: no embedding provider available` branch that runs keyword search, expands conversational queries into keywords, merges results, and returns scored results even with no provider. ([`extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts:341`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe/extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts#L341), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
- **Tests cover FTS-only indexing/search mode:** The FTS-only test suite mocks `provider: null` and verifies forced reindexing preserves and refreshes indexed chunks in no-provider mode. ([`extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.fts-only-reindex.test.ts:10`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe/extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.fts-only-reindex.test.ts#L10), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
- **Changelog shows both features landed previously:** Changelog entries record local `node-llama-cpp` embeddings support and later FTS fallback/query expansion for memory search. ([`CHANGELOG.md:6423`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe/CHANGELOG.md#L6423), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))

So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.

Review notes: reviewed against [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a); fix evidence: commit [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a).

title: [Feature Request] memory_search: support local file search without external API
